framework.name: Multi-Agent Mutual Authentication Acknowledged But Not Solved
framework.description: Paper explicitly names multi-agent verification as 'paramount' (§2.1.3) but only solves it for two agents representing known human principals — hospital agent talks to insurance agent. The delegation is always human₁→agent₁ and human₂→agent₂, never agent₁→agent₂→agent₃ where agent₂ sub-delegates autonomously.
framework.raw_quote: agents must also verify that they authentically represent the users or organizations they claim to represent. Mutual authentication ensures that agents can trust each other's intentions, capabilities, and authority

source.title: Authenticated Delegation and Authorized AI Agents
